summary_one_line: Input must encode a font/glyph whose modified combining class equals CCC129, reached by diverse structured bytes rather than CMP-operand substitution.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structured font input that, after shaping, yields a non-spacing-mark glyph whose modified combining class is exactly 129 (CCC129), so the switch(klass) at line 154 takes the case CCC129 true side. This is a derived integer state set deep in the shaping pipeline, not a literal byte compared at a fixed offset.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive's plain havoc/byte stack explores diverse multi-byte structured font content (winner seeds carry varied tables and string-like fields such as 97 97 97, 61 61 61 61 'aaaa', and 73 6e 2d 'sn-'), giving it a higher chance to land an input whose recategorized combining class reaches 129; naive resolved the branch in 8/10 trials.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog spends per-execution budget on CMP interception and I2S substitution, but the blocker has no input-to-state CMP edge feeding klass==129 (klass is derived from glyph property tables, not from a byte compared against a constant), so I2S substitution gains nothing here while the extra cost slows exploration; cmplog blocked in 8/10 trials and its blocking seeds are dominated by low-diversity uniform runs (08 08 08..., 44 44 44...).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The winner is naive, which has NO I2S. The branch is enabled by lucky havoc byte mutation discovering structured font content that produces combining class 129; cmplog's input-to-state substitution finds no useful CMP operand for this derived-state switch (klass is not a byte compared against a constant), so its comparison-solving machinery confers no advantage while its per-execution CMP overhead leaves it behind. This is a havoc-only win.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive>cmplog with an I2S technique delta; naive is the winner and cmplog the loser.
naive resolved the branch in 8/10 trials while cmplog blocked in 8/10.
cmplog blocked the branch in 8/10 trials.
The blocker is the CCC129 case of the switch on klass, a derived combining-class integer.
The klass value gating the blocker comes from a glyph property derived in the shaping pipeline, not a byte compared at a fixed offset.
Winner seeds carry diverse structured font content including repeated byte runs and string-like fields.
Loser (cmplog) blocking seeds are dominated by low-diversity uniform byte runs.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace works by matching a CMP operand in the input and substituting the other operand, which requires an input-to-state comparison edge to exploit.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S and relies on a havoc + token byte mutation stack.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Not applicable / not required: the decisive winner is naive (no I2S), so claimed_mechanism is havoc-only, not I2SRandReplace. Lineage dash-row verification is only mandated when the winning technique is I2SRandReplace. Here cmplog (the I2S-bearing fuzzer) is the LOSER, so there is no I2S win to verify; the win is attributed to naive's plain havoc byte mutation discovering structured font content that yields combining class 129.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating a branch on a derived combining-class-style integer (no input-to-state CMP edge) in which cmplog reaches the branch as fast as or faster than naive — that would show I2S helps here and refute the havoc-only attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The claim that no exploitable input-to-state CMP edge feeds klass==129 is inferred from the source structure (klass derived from glyph tables) rather than directly observed in CmpLog buffers.
